response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned web and full-stack engineer with over 15 years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, he has no stated Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a non-negotiable requirement. His core expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and web/application development; C programming appears only marginally (a brief NI stint writing CVI components and a student-era C++ audio project), with no evidence of UNIX systems-level C development. There is no demonstrated experience with embedded networking, Linux kernel modification, L2/L3 switch technologies, or any of the specified security domains (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, device attack detection).
strengths: Long career spanning 15+ years across diverse environments including embedded, web, and systems contexts, demonstrating broad adaptability
Some Linux familiarity and exposure to low-latency, high-performance systems (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic)
Demonstrated ownership mentality — founded projects, led open-source platforms, and has spoken at technical conferences
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement that alone disqualifies the candidate
No C/UNIX systems programming depth; career is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js/web with only superficial C exposure
No networking, embedded firmware, or security domain experience — MACsec, 802.1X, Linux kernel modification, and switch chip knowledge are all absent
